source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/01_HAD1717MC(MG)/▶ CE MDD/■ 3. 3차 CE TCF 보안사항 제출 (12월10일)/06. Clinical evaluation report/A-CER-01_Clinical Evaluation Report 20.12.10.pdf

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MG, HAD1717MC
authorityOrJurisdiction: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation report Rev.5 for HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC digital flat panel X-ray detectors, dated Dec/10/2020.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation for X-ray detector safety and efficacy based on MDD 93/42/EC amended by Directive 2007/47/EC and MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.
keyPoints: The device is a wired digital flat panel detector for general radiographic systems for human anatomy.
The report relies on scientific literature, equivalence to PEDRA_17FC and PEDRA_17FG, test reports, risk management, MAUDE, recall, and alert database review.
The report states no clinical investigation was conducted.
The report concludes the device is safe and effective without added and unknown clinical evaluation risk.

entities.name: Radisen Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: PEDRA_17FC Radisen Co., Ltd.; PEDRA_17FG Radisen Co., Ltd.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation under MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4
concepts.evidence: The selected main methods and procedures are focused on MEDDEV.2.7.1 Rev.4 (June. 2016) Clinical Evaluation

concepts.name: MDD Annex X clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: Council Directive 93/42EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC concerning medical device Annex X – Clinical evaluation

concepts.name: Digital flat panel X-ray detector intended use
concepts.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Use
concepts.evidence: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

concepts.name: Mammography contraindication
concepts.pageTarget: Mammography Exclusion for General Radiography Detectors
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography.

concepts.name: Substantial equivalence
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated that our product is substantially equivalent to the device placed on the market already.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance update of clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: The clinical evaluation and its documentation must be actively updated with data obtained from the post market.

evidence.claim: The report identifies File No. A-CER-01, Rev. No 5, dated Dec/10/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: File No A-CER-01 Rev. No 5 Date Dec/10/2020

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report covers HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
The device classification and GMDN code are Class IIa and 34167 Solid state x-ray imager.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification & GMDN code : Class Ⅱa and 34167 Solid state x-ray imager(flat panel/digital imager)

evidence.claim: The detector is wired and uses TFT glass and scintillator materials.
evidence.evidenceText: This is a wired digital flat panel detector... The detector is used in combination with a TFT glass and scintillator (CSI, GDX)

evidence.claim: The intended use is general radiographic imaging of human anatomy and replacement of film or screen radiographic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy. It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The report states the device was undergoing initial CE marking.
evidence.evidenceText: whether the device is being developed/ undergoing initial CE-marking/ is CE-marked Initial CE-marking

evidence.claim: The report states no clinical investigation was conducted.
evidence.evidenceText: This product did not conduct clinical investigation.

evidence.claim: The selected evaluation type was currently available scientific literature.
evidence.evidenceText: Type selected Scientific literature currently available ●

evidence.claim: The report identifies PEDRA_17FC and PEDRA_17FG from Radisen Co., Ltd. as equivalent devices.
evidence.evidenceText: Identification of the equivalent devices and its manufacturer... PEDRA_17FC Radisen Co., Ltd.; PEDRA_17FG Radisen Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The report concludes equivalence regarding technical, clinical, performance, biological, safety, appearance, and intended purpose.
evidence.evidenceText: our product has equivalence regarding Technical, Clinical, Performance, Biological, Safety, Appearance and intended purpose.

evidence.claim: Electrical safety and performance reports include EN60601-1, EN60601-1-2, a performance report, and EN IEC 62220-1 DQE test report.
evidence.evidenceText: EN60601-1... EN60601-1-2... Performance report... EN IEC 62220-1 DQE test report

evidence.claim: The literature search covered 2019.07.16 to 2020.03.03 and the period 2000 to 2020.03.03.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of search 2019.07.16 ~ 2020.03.03 Period covered by search: 2000 ~ 2020.03.03

evidence.claim: The report says clinical evaluation documentation should be updated with post-market data and CER revision considered yearly.
evidence.evidenceText: We collect information from delivered product such as customer feedback, adverse event, side effect and field failure with a 3months period and then consider revising CER with 1 year period.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata is ambiguous: source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document is primarily a CE MDD clinical evaluation report and also references FDA MAUDE and recall databases.
Conflicting MAUDE death evidence: one table states Death 0 records, while following text states 'Of these, 2 reports were associated with the event type death'.
OCR or pagination anomaly present: pages are shown as Page 60 / 59 and Page 61 / 59.
Some declaration and signature pages appear blank or contain signature placeholders; signed evidence is not fully visible in extracted text.
